content: I'm thinking about hosting a small party at my place this weekend. It's been a while since I've had people over since moving to Pittsburgh.
That sounds like fun! What kind of party are you planning? Something casual or more of a themed event?
Probably just casual - maybe some board games, music, and snacks. I could even show off my latest speedcubing skills.
A game night sounds perfect! You mentioned moving to Pittsburgh - how are you liking it compared to the Bay Area?
It's been great actually. The cost of living is so much better, and I love having more space. Plus I can finally afford that DJ setup I've wanted.
That's awesome! Do you have a dedicated space for your DJ equipment now?
Yeah, I converted the basement into a little studio. I've been practicing a lot between work meetings. Speaking of work, I just bought a new drone for photography.
A drone? That's exciting! What kind did you get? I bet the Pittsburgh skyline looks amazing from above.
It's a Mavic Air 2. I'm planning to shoot some footage of the bridges this weekend if the weather's good. Maybe I'll even catch some of my friends playing tennis at the park.
The Mavic Air 2 is a great choice! Tennis sounds fun - do you play often?
I try to play every weekend. It's a good way to meet people here. Oh, and I need to remember to pick up some origami paper for that workshop I signed up for next month.
You're really keeping busy with all these hobbies! The origami workshop should be interesting. Have you done origami before?
Just a little. I'm excited to learn more advanced techniques. Anyway, back to the party - any suggestions for easy snacks that won't make a huge mess?
